steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
First clause: A team led by...Ritchie collected arthropods with sweep nets, for instance, — an independent clause with the parenthetical for instance set off by commas.
Second clause: another research team...gathered them with bug vacuums — a second independent clause.

Two independent clauses joined without a coordinating conjunction need a semicolon.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Choice D: sweep nets, for instance; — commas around the parenthetical for instance, then a semicolon to join the two independent clauses.

A misplaces the semicolon. B omits all punctuation around the parenthetical. C creates a comma splice between the two main clauses.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
